To find the characteristic polynomial of the matrix $A = \left(\begin{array}{cc} -\frac{5}{2} & -\frac{5}{4} \\ 8 & \frac{27}{2} \end{array}\right)$, we use the definition:
$$ p(\lambda) = \det(A - \lambda I) $$
where $I$ is the $2 \times 2$ identity matrix.

**Step 1: Set up the matrix $A - \lambda I$.**
$$ A - \lambda I = \left(\begin{array}{cc} -\frac{5}{2} & -\frac{5}{4} \\ 8 & \frac{27}{2} \end{array}\right) - \left(\begin{array}{cc} \lambda & 0 \\ 0 & \lambda \end{array}\right) = \left(\begin{array}{cc} -\frac{5}{2} - \lambda & -\frac{5}{4} \\ 8 & \frac{27}{2} - \lambda \end{array}\right) $$

**Step 2: Calculate the determinant.**
For a $2 \times 2$ matrix $\left(\begin{array}{cc} a & b \\ c & d \end{array}\right)$, the determinant is $ad - bc$.
Here:
$a = -\frac{5}{2} - \lambda$
$b = -\frac{5}{4}$
$c = 8$
$d = \frac{27}{2} - \lambda$

$$ \det(A - \lambda I) = \left(-\frac{5}{2} - \lambda\right)\left(\frac{27}{2} - \lambda\right) - \left(-\frac{5}{4}\right)(8) $$
